name: Marcus Vesterager
answer_1_question: 1 /25 | Housing Homeowners who profit the most from rising prices should pay more in tax
answer_1_elaboration: Marcus' answer "It is unreasonable that one can earn more money by sleeping in one's bed than by going to work. In many other cases the gain is taxable, for example with shares. I do not think the housing gain should be exempt." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_2_question: 2 /25 | Defence and security Denmark spends too much money supporting Ukraine in the war against Russia
answer_2_elaboration: Marcus' answer "Of course we must support Ukraine. The alternative would be horrific, particularly for the Ukrainians but also for our own security and morality. There should of course be room to discuss how we can best provide support, and I believe the support should primarily be channelled through the EU system. It is good that Denmark contributes so much, but I believe other countries should do more." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_3_question: 3 /25 | Economy and labour market The Great Prayer Day should be reinstated as a public holiday, even if it costs the state money
answer_3_elaboration: Marcus' answer "I disagreed with the way the Great Prayer Day was abolished, because I believe the social partners should have been involved." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_4_question: 4 /25 | Justice policy It is more important to be able to expel more criminal foreigners than to comply with international conventions
answer_4_elaboration: Marcus' answer "We are clearly at a turning point where individual elements of the international conventions will likely soon be reformulated. These conventions are cornerstones of our domestic political system too, and we must not neglect them, but change them from within." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_5_question: 5 /25 | Social affairs and inequality There should be greater equalisation between wealthy and poor municipalities
answer_5_elaboration: Marcus' answer "I believe equalisation is both a way of ensuring a fair distribution of the country's resources and a necessity for maintaining cohesion." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_6_question: 6 /25 | Economy and labour market The state pension age should continue to rise by one year when average life expectancy rises by one year
answer_6_elaboration: Marcus' answer "In the future there will be many more people in their 70s who are still in the labour market, and good for them. But there is a big difference in what jobs one can perform after a long life. At the same time, one must also respect that people are not put on this earth to work, and that one has the right to time off, especially in retirement." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_7_question: 7 /25 | Tax Tax on the highest incomes should be raised
answer_7_elaboration: Marcus' answer "There is growing inequality in Denmark, and it risks challenging our sense of community. The very wealthiest can contribute more to society than they do today. I believe that is classic social democratic policy." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_8_question: 8 /25 | Development aid Denmark should spend less money on development aid
answer_8_elaboration: Marcus' answer "We should spend more but in the right way. Development aid contributes to poverty reduction and creates better local conditions. I also believe we have a duty to help the poorest. When development aid is used correctly it can also help fulfil our national political interests. It can strengthen Danish companies and reduce the incentive to migrate towards Europe." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_9_question: 9 /25 | Climate, environment and agriculture Nuclear power on Danish soil should be part of the energy supply in the longer term
answer_9_elaboration: Marcus' answer "It is expensive and takes a long time. If the technology becomes cheaper and safer in the future, we should of course reconsider. We should rather focus on what we are best at: wind and solar." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_10_question: 10 /25 | Economy and labour market Benefits for the poorest welfare recipients should be raised
answer_10_elaboration: Marcus' answer "Our society is wealthy enough to help those who are struggling. We can of course set requirements for welfare recipients, but the state must not contribute to pushing anyone into poverty." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_11_question: 11 /25 | School and education More children with special needs should attend special education classes rather than mainstream school classes
answer_11_elaboration: Marcus' answer "Children with special needs should, as far as possible, be part of an equal community of children, but unfortunately there are far too many examples of this creating inappropriate situations for all parties if there is no room for the necessary educational effort. The inclusion project is a cost-cutting exercise that needs to be restored. That can only happen if more resources are allocated." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_12_question: 12 /25 | Climate, environment and agriculture It should be prohibited for agriculture to spray in vulnerable drinking water areas, even if it may make food production more expensive
answer_12_elaboration: Marcus' answer "We must find other ways to reduce the price of food. It simply cannot be allowed to compromise our drinking water and the health of future generations." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_13_question: 13 /25 | Transport It is more important to invest in trains and buses than in motorways
answer_13_elaboration: Marcus' answer "Public transport deserves a significant boost, and far too much money is being spent on increasing road capacity for motorists, which simply generates more cars. If one needs to travel from Aalborg to Copenhagen or from Hvidovre to Østerbro, public transport should be the most natural choice. In the Capital Region, we need an express tunnel to Rigshospitalet that will strengthen the entire metro network." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_14_question: 14 /25 | Social affairs and inequality It is acceptable for economic inequality to grow, as long as everyone becomes wealthier
answer_14_elaboration: Marcus' answer "It is not a problem that some people become wealthy and some wealthier than others. But it is a huge problem when economic inequality increases, and it has done so for a number of years." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_15_question: 15 /25 | Health The price of cigarettes should be significantly raised
answer_15_elaboration: Marcus' answer "It should be a public health goal that no new smokers emerge. It harms both the smoker themselves but also those around them." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_16_question: 16 /25 | Transport Taxes on petrol and diesel should be reduced
answer_16_elaboration: Marcus' answer "The fuel of the future must not be fossil-based, but green and sustainable." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_17_question: 17 /25 | Economy and labour market Politicians should work towards an agreement to reduce working hours, even if it comes at an economic cost to society and individuals
answer_17_elaboration: Marcus' answer "I have great sympathy for the idea that one finds greater meaning in life by being with the people one loves than by being at work, and we must be willing to have the conversation about whether we can organise the labour market differently. But our community rests on a mutual understanding of duty and rights, and it is economically unsustainable if we do not accept that contract." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_18_question: 18 /25 | Immigration Applicants for Danish citizenship should be screened for anti-democratic views
answer_18_elaboration: Marcus' answer "To become a Danish citizen, we as a society can allow ourselves to expect that one shares some fundamental values that strongly characterise Denmark. We can probably not fully ensure that a screening will be sufficient to guarantee this, but it should nonetheless be part of the citizenship process." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_19_question: 19 /25 | Climate, environment and agriculture When installing solar panels, consideration for the local population should weigh more heavily than the green transition
answer_19_elaboration: Marcus' answer "Local residents must be involved, and it should be possible to receive compensation or to purchase a share in the solar panels to ensure local ownership and support. But local objections must not dictate the installation of solar panels." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_20_question: 20 /25 | Economy and labour market The door should be opened to more foreign labour from countries outside Europe and North America
answer_20_elaboration: Marcus' answer "We need labour, and there are skilled people all over the world who can contribute positively across a range of different jobs. This does not mean they necessarily need to live here for the rest of their lives, and it does not mean we should not consider who has the best prerequisites for becoming part of society." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_21_question: 21 /25 | School and education It should be easier to expel pupils from their state school if their behaviour causes problems
answer_21_elaboration: Marcus' answer "The state school is for the community, and each individual pupil must be able to adapt. If the pupil cannot do that, alternative methods must be used to ensure it does not affect the other pupils. However, this individual pupil must be met with help and support." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_22_question: 22 /25 | Tax VAT on food should be reduced
answer_22_elaboration: Marcus' answer "It would be particularly appropriate to reduce VAT on selected foods, and here I am thinking of fruit and vegetables and organic products." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_23_question: 23 /25 | Culture The state should cut its support for the Danish Broadcasting Corporation (DR)
answer_23_elaboration: Marcus' answer "It is important to have a shared objective news medium. The shared public sphere is a prerequisite for our democracy." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_24_question: 24 /25 | Government formation It would be best if a government were formed across the political centre
answer_24_elaboration: Marcus' answer "We must hold fast to the Danish norm of broad cooperation. My priority will naturally be that the government is shaped by left-wing and green politics." - Marcus Vesterager
answer_25_question: 25 /25 | Elderly care It is more important to be able to find enough staff for elder care than whether they can speak Danish
answer_25_elaboration: Marcus' answer "The vast majority of elderly people would probably prefer to receive the necessary care, regardless of whether they can speak with the staff. At the same time, there will be colleagues who can help with the language barriers, and over time these new staff will also learn to speak Danish." - Marcus Vesterager
